title: 修复 SBOM 语义、漏洞关联与发布信任链
outOfScope: private signing key creation or storage
changes to historical archived Work Items

sources.reason: Current lock parser, SBOM builder, and vulnerability scanner described in the remediation report.
Existing supply-chain regression tests and reported missing assertions.
Current release trust-chain limitation and documented distribution contract.
Generated CycloneDX baseline currently containing malformed versions.
Generated provenance baseline tied to SBOM digest.
Release digest metadata must remain consistent with generated evidence.

riskAssessment.reason: The change alters generated release evidence and the identity mapping used by vulnerability reporting.

executionDecision.reason: The user approved the remediation design; implementation and verification are in scope.

preReviewWarnings: Review generated CycloneDX validity, stable component identity, vulnerability-to-bom-ref mapping, and the distinction between repository consistency and an external signature trust root.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Lock versions are normalized without a trailing continuation character and each locked package's hashes are represented on its SBOM component.
The generated document is valid CycloneDX 1.5 with stable bom-ref/purl identity, component hashes, metadata, and dependency edges.
pip-audit vulnerability records are mapped to SBOM component bom-ref values and fail closed when a finding cannot be mapped.
Release evidence includes a verifiable digest manifest and documentation distinguishes internal consistency from an external trust root.
Regression tests cover the reported P0 and the new identity/trust-chain behavior.
guidelines: Generated evidence is produced by commands and is not hand-edited.
Do not add private keys, credentials, or machine-specific configuration to the template.
Document the boundary between repository digest consistency and independently verifiable release signatures.

intent.problem: The current supply-chain checker emits malformed package versions, loses artifact hashes, builds an incomplete hand-written CycloneDX document, and reports pip-audit findings without a component identity. Repository-local release digests are internally consistent but are not independently verifiable as a publisher trust root.
constraints: Use the already locked cyclonedx-python-lib dependency.
Keep source-commit evidence resolution backward compatible.
Do not create or store private signing keys in the template.
Generate evidence files through the existing command entry point.
intent.rationale: Fixing the data model at the lock-parser boundary and using CycloneDX's typed serializer makes the SBOM and vulnerability mapping share stable identities. A generated digest manifest closes repository-internal consistency while documentation preserves the distinction from external signatures or attestations.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: vulnerability cannot map to SBOM component

scenarioCoverage.reason: No signing key or external publishing service is in repository scope; documentation records this residual boundary.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly approved implementation of the review-backed supply-chain remediation, including generated evidence and Makefile checks.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
